widgets_values:
STYLE: cinematic, realistic, natural motion, coherent lighting.
SUBJECT: a woman florist in her late 40s with kind eyes and a warm smile.
SCENE: narrow florist shop at opening time, colorful bouquets, soft morning light through the front glass, dust motes in the air.
ACTION: the camera weaves between flowers and the florist's hands as she trims stems, unwraps fresh flowers, fills a bucket with water, and notices a customer entering.
CAMERA: close-up handheld shot, intimate framing, slow pan at the end toward flowers and the open front door.
LIGHTING: soft warm morning light, gentle highlights on petals, natural shadows.
AUDIO: distinct scissors snipping, paper crinkling, water filling a bucket, faint front-door chime, subtle street ambience.
DIALOGUE: the florist looks into the camera and says gently, "Can't beat the smell of fresh cut flowers."
ENDING: camera slowly pans to flowers catching the morning light while the door opens wider, revealing the street outside.

nodes.title: Credits
LTX 2.3 AV Workflow — Clean Professional Layout
Resolution Presets — next to Resolution Source
widgets_values:
Resolution control:
- Source Canvas + Scale By controls final latent size.
- Current: 1536x1024 with scale 0.5 = 768x512.
Suggested presets:
• 1024x1024 + 0.5 = 512x512
• 1536x1024 + 0.5 = 768x512
• 1024x1536 + 0.5 = 512x768
• 1792x1024 + 0.5 = 896x512
Keep dimensions divisible by 32.

nodes.title: Duration Presets — next to Frame Count
widgets_values:
Duration selector:
Duration = Frames / FPS.
At 24 FPS:
• 72 frames ≈ 3 sec
• 121 frames ≈ 5 sec
• 192 frames ≈ 8 sec
• 240 frames ≈ 10 sec
• 360 frames ≈ 15 sec, heavy
Recommended RTX 4090 cap: 240 frames / 10 sec for reliability.

nodes.title: LoRA Usage — next to LoRA Controls
widgets_values:
LoRA handling:
- Active path uses the Base Speed/Distilled LoRA only.
- Optional detail LoRA slots are disabled and disconnected to avoid accidental stacking.
- To use one, connect: Base Speed/Distilled LoRA → Optional Detail LoRA Slot → Generation Step1 model input.
- Avoid stacking many LoRAs unless you specifically need the effect.

nodes.title: Model Loading Notes
widgets_values:
Model files usually do not need changes.

UNet: LTX 2.3 22B GGUF Q8.
CLIP: Gemma 3 + LTX text projection.
Video/Audio VAE must match LTX 2.3.

Only swap these if you have the matching model files.

nodes.title: Prompt Structure Notes
widgets_values:
Write the prompt like a short film brief:

STYLE → SUBJECT → SCENE → ACTION
CAMERA → LIGHTING → AUDIO → DIALOGUE → ENDING

For best results, describe the sound and dialogue explicitly.

nodes.title: Negative Prompt Notes
widgets_values:
Keep this focused on quality problems.

Useful for reducing flicker, bad lip sync, watermarks, subtitles, distorted hands/faces, and audio artifacts.

Avoid adding too many unrelated negatives.

nodes.title: Conditioning Notes
widgets_values:
This applies the FPS-aware LTXV conditioning.

The FPS value here should match your intended output FPS.

If video/audio timing feels off, check FPS and frame count first.

nodes.title: Latent Input Notes
widgets_values:
Video and audio latents must use the same frame count and compatible FPS.

Current setup targets about 5 seconds.

For longer clips, increase frames carefully because VRAM and render time grow quickly.

nodes.title: Generation Notes
widgets_values:
Two-stage generation:

Step 1 creates the base video/audio latent and performs latent spatial upscale.
Step 2 refines the result.

Keep CFG conservative for better stability.

nodes.title: Decode Notes
widgets_values:
Video and audio are separated, then decoded independently.

Tiled video decode reduces memory pressure.

If decode fails, reduce resolution or frame count first.

nodes.title: Export / Preview Notes
widgets_values:
SaveVideo is intentionally large so the preview pane is easier to inspect.

MP4 output is created after video frames and audio are combined.

Use lower CRF for quality, higher CRF for smaller files.
